linkedin_profile.headline: Head of Guest Experience at Fulham Pier
linkedin_profile.about:
I am a visitor experience specialist and creative director who has launched several major attractions and location based entertainment venues. My hallmark is building successful and dynamic teams that deliver exceptional standards of service. I have overseen the entertainment and experience propositions at four major London tourist destinations and I also oversaw events and VIP experiences at Hamleys of London. In July 2022 I devised and launched Wyvernwood (TripAdvisor Travellers Choice Award Winner 2024); a 27-acre immersive adventure park in North Essex and have recently assumed the role of Head of Guest Experience at Fulham Pier; a unique new destination positioned along the River Thames.

Total Experience: 17 years and 8 months

experience.title: Performance Trainer
experience.description: The No 1 visitor attraction operator in Europe, No 2 in the world behind Disney employing 21,000 staff worldwide. Merlin currently run over 100 attractions and operate over 4 continents. In my role as a Trainer: • Responsible for training new actors to perform over 20 different character roles for up to 4000 people per day to the highest quality whilst meeting with brand specs and strict time limits. • Created and introduced a unique statistic based incentive scheme that recorded achievements and recognition across all departments, boosting team morale. • Oversaw the much-publicised move from the famous Tooley Street site to the new flagship venue in County Hall in March 2013. • Created and ran practical workshops to help actors maintain energy, motivation and physical/vocal strength, as well as helping them deal with conflict management, health & safety and customer care.

experience.title: PR Media Captain For GB And Western Europe
experience.description: Diageo is a multinational alcoholic beverages company based in London. It is the world's largest producer of spirits. As PR & Media Captain: ·The face of Captain Morgan for the 2011 re-branding and re-launch of Captain Morgan’s Spiced in UK markets and also its introduction in Belgium. ·Create exceptional experiences for celebrity and high profile clientele at major media events. ·Present YouTube broadcasts, entertaining and improvising with the public at events throughout Europe and for various UK magazines and newspapers. ·Comprehensive media training by Ogilvy PR to conform with Diageo Marketing Code. ·Attend sales conferences throughout Europe to give speeches and presentations to both Diageo employees and clients. ·Training other Captains to make regional appearances at smaller scale venues.

experience.title: Performance Team Leader
experience.description: As Performance Team Leader: • Part of the initial management team who launched this unique attraction on London’s South Bank. • Helped ensure the efficient operational running of the performance department through consistent delivery of performance, monitoring standards and giving constructive feedback and support when necessary and any other supporting administrative duties. • Maintained exceptionally high theatrical performance standards for the Performance Department, with show creation, costume standards and show specification monitoring. • Guided training and workshops. • Supported and enhanced the unique ‘visitor experience’ with show proposals and creation. • Ensured the smooth running of shows on a day to day basis by acting as the Duty Performance representative. • Engaged with actors and costume team on a daily basis, dealing with any problems, arising issues and other matters that are of relevance to the department whilst promoting honesty, integrity and professionalism. • Helped co-ordinate actor auditions on a regular basis to allow for the recruitment of talented and motivated employees.

experience.title: Experience And Entertainment Manager
experience.description: Hamleys is the oldest and largest toy shop in the world and one of the world's best-known retailers of toys. My role: • Responsible for overseeing the experience in-store for the millions of visitors to Hamleys each year by providing world class entertainment, creativity and discipline across all areas. An active ambassador of the company’s brand image. • Responsible for the delivery of all seasonal, corporate and marketing events in store to ensure that they are executed professionally at all times from conception to completion. • Providing a high level of customer experience to guests who require a personal shopper, birthday party or VIP package; including overseeing private hire for the entire venue. Aiming to maximise corporate, personal shops and party income whilst actively seeking new opportunities and ensuring the highest standards of implementation in store. • Creating and implementing all in-store theatre including Christmas at Hamleys, the launch of ‘Harry Potter at Hamleys’ and ‘Frozen II at Hamleys’ as well as various seasonal shows.

experience.title: Experiences Director
experience.description: Wyvernwood - The Enchanted Lost Kingdom, is a 27-acre outdoor immersive adventure park which launched in Alresford, Essex in the Summer of 2022. TripAdvisor Travellers Choice Award Winner 2024. My role: - Responsible for overseeing the creative development of Wyvernwood; project managing its launch and conceptualising the park’s design, guest journey and daily experiences. - Responsible for recruiting all seasonal park team-members and training them to deliver an exceptional guest experience. - Devised and implemented all entertainment and activities within the park from initial concept through to installation and launch. - Responsible for the delivery of all seasonal events onsite, including corporate events, private hires, school trips and birthday parties. - Working closely with the Managing Director and key stakeholders to achieve annual targets and KPIs. - Being a main point of contact with all third-party contractors to ensure projects are delivered on time and on budget.

experience.title: Head Of Guest Experience
experience.description: Fulham Pier is London's newest riverside destination · Positioned along the Thames and nestled by parkland, we’ve transformed this pocket of South-West London into a unique retreat. My role: • A key strategic lead in coordinating multiple service offerings; including hotel, restaurants, members club, creche, spa, conference & events, and entertainment venues. • Lead and inspire the guest experience team to deliver seamless, high-quality customer service across all touchpoints, both in-venue and via digital channels. • Championing a guest-centric culture focused on creating memorable and meaningful moments throughout the entire visitor journey. • Coordinating closely with cross-functional teams—including F&B, security, guest relations, maintenance, and events—to support the entire guest journey. • Serve as the escalation point for complex guest complaints, resolving issues effectively to maintain customer satisfaction and brand integrity. • Ensuring inclusivity and accessibility across the venue as well as via communication and booking channels. • Responsible for maintaining strong ties with the local community, BID partners and local authorities. • Maintaining operational readiness during high-pressure scenarios, including multiple event on-sales and peak guest flow periods. • Ensuring optimal presentation, cleanliness, safety, and accessibility across all areas of the venue, driving continuous improvements; identifying and mitigating service delivery risks, ensuring uninterrupted and exceptional experiences for all guests. • Monitoring and sharing guest sentiment, reviews, and feedback with all teams to drive service excellence and engagement strategies. Integrating AI and traditional tools to enhance guest engagement. • Coaching and mentoring staff to uphold the highest standards in service, communication, and operational responsiveness during complex and fast-paced situations.

experience.title: Waitress

experience.title: Kitchen Restaurant Trainee
experience.description: I gained knowledge in wine tasting, wine pairing, health and safety, menu planning. I spent my time to learn the basics of restaurant operations in both front and back of house, where I practiced waitressing, bartending, hostess and commis chef. - Setting up tables, preparing before lunch service - Serving table of 1 to 30 guests - Making drinks and preparing cocktail of the days - Learning new menu - Preparing, cooking and presenting food in different sections - starters, mains and desserts, under supervision of the head chef. I was able to build on my teamwork and communication skills. I also learn to take responsibility, problem-solving and time-managing skills, all those that are important for my future career.

experience.title: Guest Relations Officer
experience.organization: Shangri-La Hotel, At The Shard, London
experience.description: 1-year placement programme where I worked in a full-time position as a Guest Relations Officer. I was introduced to Hotel Operating Systems such as OPERA, ALICE, Connect, TrustYou... I also perfected Forbes training standards, including arrival, departure, guest handlings and communication. I was able to develop various abilities, technical and interpersonal skills through different duties: - Customer service, listening: exchanging guests email pre, during and post-stay, arranging amenities for different purposes of stay, answering phone calls, in-room check-in and special care for VIP guests (FHR, Diamond/ Jade member, CEOs...). - Decision-making, problem-solving, resilient and empathy: dealing with guests' requests and complaints, reporting back to my Guest Relations Manager. - Attention to details: decorating rooms and events to the highest standards. - Computing, Microsoft office, analytical: analysing daily VIP report, arrival report 7 to 1 days before guests' arrivals. - Teamwork, communication, flexibility: cooperating with different internal departments daily such as Housekeeping, In-room dining, Concierge and Marketing, as well as external such as Travel Agents. My tasks also cover Receptionist with check-in, check-out, room assignment, dealing with payment, billing, guests' complaints, updating profile and credit checks. I also took initiative to learn and cross-trained in various departments such as Finance, Communication, Reservation, Housekeeping, Events and Restaurant. I was willing to cover shifts for many positions such as Restaurant/Bar hostess, In-room dining server, Housekeeping attendance and Concierge. I have gained a lot of experience during this time and achieved monthly rewards for excellent customer services and reviews.

experience.title: Guest Relations Coordinator

With my previous experience in the same role, I continue to build on important skills: - Communication, listening, teamwork and professionalism: in charge of coordinating my colleagues, working as a team to deliver pleasant and smooth stays to our guests. - Task delegation, multi-tasking, organizing: distribute daily tasks, handle phone calls/ emails, arrival reports, as well as assist the front desk with check-in/out during rush hours. - Flexibility and adaptability: I cover shifts for Receptionist, In-room dining server and Housekeeping when needed.

experience.description: The Montcalm Collection is a group of independent luxury hotels located throughout London. The collection includes four main properties: Montcalm East in Shoreditch, Montcalm Marble Arch in Marylebone, Montcalm Royal London House in Finsbury Square, and Montcalm Brewery in Barbican. Each hotel within the Montcalm Collection has its own unique story and design, blending classic and contemporary elements to reﬂect the character of London. Montcalm Brewery is a historic former brewery transformed into a ﬁve-star hotel, while Montcalm Royal London House is a mid-century marvel with a stunning rooftop terrace. The hotels are known for their thoughtful touches, luxurious comfort, and exceptional personal service. Montcalm East is an Autograph Collection hotel with a mission to support its vibrant neighbourhood of East London. In addition to the Montcalm hotels, the collection also includes the: Montcalm Townhouses, Inhabit Hotels (member of Design Hotels) and the Park Grand Hotels. Inhabit Hotels are urban wellness properties in West London. Wellbeing is at the heart of what we do, as we offer guests both restorative and invigorating experiences to foster mindfulness, healthy habits, and even better sleep. We are committed to enhancing green initiatives by monitoring and reducing overall consumption levels and converting environmental efforts into sustainable business opportunities. Social benchmarks are likewise core to our strategic decision-making. Inhabit Hotels have been awarded B Corp™ Certification, reﬂecting our commitment to social and environmental sustainability. Park Grand Hotels are contemporary boutique hotels located in prime locations throughout London. Montcalm Collection provides a range of luxury accommodations in London. Website http://montcalmcollection.com

linkedin_profile.headline: Operations Manager at Bespoke Hotels
linkedin_profile.about: I have worked both front of house and back of house, covering kitchens, offices, bars and restaurants. I have always adopted a 'you don't know until you try' attitude, which has meant that during my brief career and journey so far, I have learnt so much about what suits me as an individual, and used this knowledge to tailor my hard working attitude towards everything I do. I give everything my all and reflect as I go along as to how to improve and how to develop from tasks that I have completed. I fully intend to continue with this view during my career and personal and professionable development for years to come.

experience.title: Waitress
experience.description: At only a mile up the lane from my parent's home, the Cefn Mably Arms was the perfect weekend job during my A-Level years. It gave me a link to the industry I had fallen for and was working towards in my studies and university applications, keeping the motivation high. My role consisted of advising customers on menu choices, allergens and drinks, as well and serving their meals in a non-intrusive and efficient manner. The waiting team were also responsible for plating the desserts that had been pre-prepared by the chef. In a designated area, we would portion, heat and plate orders, again, ensuring the highest standard was met for your guests. It was a hugely popular pub and often we found ourselves full to the rafters and w

experience.title: You Niversity Plus Placement Student
experience.description: The 3rd year of my degree was the 'sandwich' aspect. We were required to find and complete a minimum of a 40 week placement within the industry. PPHE hired me in their hotel Park Plaza Victoria, on their you:niversity plus placement programme. I worked in the food and beverage team, rotating between the breakfast service, bar team and room service. After roughly three months in this role, I was lucky enough to be promoted to a supervisor. This role not only involved me working on the floor and heading up the teams during service, but also ensuring time sheets were completed and financial and cover forecasts were submitted each month for budget. The rough hours, limited time off and long days meant I found this role particularly taxing. During the peak summer months, some days breakfast would reach 400 covers! I thoroughly enjoyed my time at Park Plaza, and made some great friends, however most importantly, it taught me a lot about myself and what roles/positions suit me the most and allow me to excel. After this role, I stepped away from the operational side of hospitality and went back for my final year of my degree with the focus on the office side of the industry.

experience.title: Work Experience
experience.description: A month after finishing my placement year with Park Plaza, I found myself intrigued as to how another hotel chain conducts itself. Using existing contacts, I was able to organise a weeks experience in Hilton Cardiff. I spent the week rotating between departments and understanding and observing their various day-to-day tasks. I started the week with housekeeping, which was the most eye opening experience for me from the whole week. It made me realise how little I appreciated their role in the past and how important they are to the functioning of the hotel. Without them, there would be no ready rooms for guests. I then worked for a day in guest relations, understanding what exactly it takes to ensure that the stay is a smooth as possible and how to make the guest feel valued and appreciated. Finally, I helped HR conduct a recruitment day, to which over 150 applicant attended. I thoroughly enjoyed this day as it really played to my advantage of being a people person! The week was incredibly interesting and experiencing another chain hotel certainly gave me a lot to think about.

experience.title: Placement PAL Leader
experience.description: During my final year of studies at Bournemouth University, I took it upon myself to work for the Placement PAL scheme. It was put in place to guide those who are searching for a placement and to support any queries or worries they may have and the year that I worked for the scheme was it's first year. I chose to help this scheme as it was something that I definitely would've found comforting and would have benefited from when I was experiencing placement applications. I feel that in situations such as placement applications, talking it through with a peer who has recently gone through the same can be extremely comforting and can provide such peace of mind.

experience.title: Bar Maid
experience.description: After finish my degree and graduating with a 2:1, I moved temporarily back to Cardiff whilst I looked for a job. The Cefn Mably Arms, where I spent a couple of years working before university, was under new ownership and had a massive refurbishment. I approached them and told them about my history with the pub and how locally I lived, I was hired instantly. I loved being back at the pub and continuing my work in hospitality, whilst rebuilding my relationship with the same regulars as my last job there. I worked mainly on the bar, however stepping into the restaurant when needed. After a month back, the landlord and landlady approached me and asked if I would consider being their new manager full time. Unfortunately at this point, I had a few leads for jobs in London, and had my heart set on going back to work in London, I felt the role would be better in someone else's hands, so I politely turned it down and hoped they understood. They were wonderful and kindly made me a shift leader for the remainder of the summer until I left for full time work and life in London.

experience.title: Sales Management Programme
experience.organization: Red Carnation Hotels - part of the TTC family of brands
experience.description: The Sales Management Programme within Red Carnation Hotels is a unique experience of a year long rotational position. You cover departments such as conference and events and reservations to prepare yourself for a sales role. Having the experience of two very different departments results in an all-rounded sales team member. Having the knowledge to sell event spaces, bedrooms and the hotels as a whole is an effective tool and results in confident sales calls. My time begun at The Montague on the Gardens in conference and events, my main project being the indivudal 'ski pass' reservation system for their unique christmas pop-up, The Ski Lodge. After 5 wonderful months loving this role, I moved across the office to work with the reservations team. A very different experience! The work is focused and the time scale reduced. Your actions directly impact a guests' stay and therefore there can be some pressure. It took a while to grasp as it was so new to me but evenutally I found my stride and even managed to get a great result on the AA auditor. Again, after 5 months, it was time for me to move on. I was asked if I would step into a unique position, a bi-monthly switch of jobs. Currently I spend one month being a Sales Executive at The Chesterfield Mayfair, lookng after mainly corporate accounts and selling the fabulous property. The next month I go on the road with our Regional Director of Sales and look more at the leisure and conference and events markets for the UK hotels. I switch month to month between the two, keeping me on my toes! I am looking forward to a bright future with the company.

WEBSITE • Developed and managed website, pages, content, and materials to ensure The Mandrake brand is effectively promoted to the defined target audiences. • Updating the website with AiR, Wellbeing, special offers, improving efficiency of site, troubleshooting, improving SEO PR • Managing all Travel, F&B, wellness & lifestyle PR for the hotel SOCIAL MEDIA • Curating and implementing a social media strategy • Increased social media followers by 220% within 6 months

linkedin_profile.headline: Chief Executive Officer at THE LANCASTER LANDMARK HOTEL COMPANY LIMITED
linkedin_profile.about: Fergus commenced his role with Jumeirah Hotels and Resorts in August 2019. Initially overseeing the European portfolio with an emphasis on the closure of Jumeirah Carlton Towers in Knightsbridge and kick starting the re-positioning and reopening of this iconic hotel. In a pre planned transfer he moved to Dubai in January 2020 Jumeirah is a global luxury hotel company and operates and manages a world-class portfolio of 15 properties in the Middle East including the flagship Burj Al Arab, 5 properties in Europe and 4 in Asia with 10 properties currently under construction around the globe. Prior to this Fergus worked for Intercontinental Hotel Group joining them in March 2008 as General Manager of the ANA Intercontinental Tokyo. In July 2009 he was promoted to Area General Manager, then to Regional General Manager in November 2010 and oversaw all properties in the Greater Tokyo Area. In 2012 Fergus was promoted to Director of Operations Thailand and Indochina and subsequently took up the role as Head of Japan and CEO of the joint venture IHGANA in January 2014. In January 2016 Fergus took up the role as Vice President Middle East and in September 2017 he was promoted to Vice President Luxury Division Europe. Fergus trained at the Central Hotel Glasgow and started his career at the Grosvenor House Hotel in London. Prior to his current London based role he has spent almost 25 years working extensively overseas in 11 different countries including Japan, Hong Kong, Singapore, Thailand and the UAE. Prior to joining IHG Fergus’s extensive operational experience in these countries was gained mainly from working with the Shangri-la and Hyatt organisations. Originally from Scotland, Fergus obtained the UK Diploma in Hotel Catering and Institutional Operations as well as the Cornell Executive Program in Hospitality Management through the National University of Singapore. Fergus is married with 2 daughters aged 18 and 16. He is a keen golfer and follower of rugby and football.

experience.title: General Manager
experience.description:
Company: 201-500 employees • Founded 2025 • Privately Held • Hospitality
Six Senses London will open with 109 guest rooms and suites and 14 branded residences offering access to the hotel’s facilities and all the privileges that come with a Six Senses home, including bespoke interior design, concierge service, dining, housekeeping, and wellness programming. There’s a cozy lobby bar and lounge (for talking to friends or the plants), an all-day dining restaurant with an open kitchen, and a seating area in the courtyard. Six Senses Spa will mirror the different energies of the city life, from the sensory stimulation and movement of the street to the stillness and calm of a quiet café or park. Highlights include 3,500 square feet (325 square meters) dedicated to fitness, a 65-foot (20-meter) indoor swimming pool, the Alchemy Bar, and a relaxation room with vaulted ceilings. On the second floor, residents and members will access a new kind of social and wellness club featuring a central bar and lounge, co-working spaces, a restaurant, and wellness and treatment rooms. It will offer pioneering programming to encourage growth, reflection and reconnection.
Department: C-Suite • Level: Manager

experience.title: Director Of Sales Marketing
experience.description: Six Senses London will open with 109 guest rooms and suites and 14 branded residences offering access to the hotel’s facilities and all the privileges that come with a Six Senses home, including bespoke interior design, concierge service, dining, housekeeping, and wellness programming. There’s a cozy lobby bar and lounge (for talking to friends or the plants), an all-day dining restaurant with an open kitchen, and a seating area in the courtyard. In a space reminiscent of an old-fashioned London underground station, Six Senses Spa will mirror the different energies of the city life, from the sensory stimulation and movement of the street to the stillness and calm of a quiet café or park. Highlights include 3,500 square feet (325 square meters) dedicated to fitness, a 65-foot (20-meter) indoor swimming pool, the Alchemy Bar, and a relaxation room with vaulted ceilings. On the second floor, residents and members will access a new kind of social and wellness club featuring a central bar and lounge, coworking spaces, a restaurant, and wellness and treatment rooms. It will offer pioneering programming to encourage growth, reflection and reconnection.

linkedin_profile.headline: Director of Technical Services at The Whiteley & Six Senses
linkedin_profile.about:
I am an effective Director of Engineering with over nine years’ experience running maintenance operations and large projects at a leading London hotel. I am customer-focused and tenacious with experience across project management, team leadership, electrical engineering and waste management. I pride myself on excellent stakeholder management, workflow coordination and communication skills, managing large teams of internal staff and external contractors. I am an effective project leader and problem-solver with advanced programming skills and an ability to work with minimal direction to solve problems, resolve conflicts and respond to customer queries. Specialities: Project Management,Energy Management, Hotel Maintenance,Health and Safety & Compliance, Team Leadership, Customer Service.

Total Experience: 14 years and 2 months

experience.title: Mechanical Engineer
experience.description: In this role, I managed the repairs and maintenance of plant rooms, including air handling units, cooling towers and pool plant rooms, managing stock and ordering relevant parts needed for continuity of service with minimum disruption. I assisted the lead engineer with laundry equipment maintenance upkeep including boilers, washing machines, tumble drivers and automated machines, ensuing everything ran in line with expectations.

experience.title: Technical Quality Control Supervisor
experience.description: During this role, I maintained and updated an inventory of faults in the building, managing employees and contractors towards resolution at the highest standard, handing over rooms to hotel operations upon completion. I also developed the engineering department, negotiating a service agreement and supporting the Director of Engineering with the drafting and agreement of the yearly budget, ensuring sufficient flexibility for issue resolution.

experience.title: Assistant Director Of Engineering
experience.description: In this role, I managed the day-to-day operation of the 160-room hotel including guest rooms, spa, public areas, meeting rooms and plant rooms, managing 20 engineers across various tasks and ensuring tasks were appropriately allocated. Highlights from my role include: • Delivered training and development programmes for the engineers, ensuring relevant regulations were met • Undertook waste management and delivered an energy-efficient approach to running of plants and equipment • Managed the engineering budget and stock required to run the hotel with minimal levels of disruption

experience.title: Project Manager
experience.description: During this role, I planned and executed a £7m refurbishment project, converting the ground floor of the hotel from a restaurant and reception space into a new grand modern lobby space and library, as well as conversion of a members lounge and smaller serving pantry into a new all-day dining restaurant space with a central show kitchen. Highlights from my role include: • Led the tender process, analysing various offers and choosing contractors best suited to delivering a complex project, ensuring minimal disruption to hotel guests during the renovation period, as the hotel remained open • Liaised with the mechanical, electrical, plumbing and lighting consultant as well as structural engineers, architects and interior designers, in order to ensure development progressed in line with overall strategic objectives • Managed health and safety site compliance, ensuring that the refurbishment met all relevant regulations

experience.title: Director Of Engineering
experience.description: In this role, I manage a team of 20 engineers at the hotel, with an overall £2.4m budget, which is currently running £35k under. I this role I support team training and development, negotiating a £30k annual budget specifically for training courses. Highlights from my role include: • Improved the health and safety compliance percentage significantly from 77% to 91%, within a one-year period • Led a £250k project to replace the lighting for all 160 guest rooms, retrofitting new energy-saving LED lighting • Oversaw a £350k styling and artwork project in the guest rooms and public areas, from purchasing to installation

experience.title: Director Of Technical Services At Six Senses London
experience.description: I am happy to share that my role at The Whiteley Estate is expanding and I have joined the pre opening team for Six senses. I am so proud to work for companies like Savills and Six Senses who share very similar values.
